messages.content:
Translate to English

"A tecnologia chegou a níveis nunca antes imaginados e agora podemos nós mesmos compor trilhas novas pro seriado Chaves"
"Technology has reached levels never before imagined, and now we can compose new tracks for the Chaves TV show ourselves."

Translate to Japanese (with Romanization)

"I Found Her"

"I Found Her" 

Romanization: "Watashi wa kanojo o mitsukemashita" (私は彼女を見つけました)

Translate to Japanese

"My wife is so awesome! I'm going to write a self-insert of her!"

私の妻はとても素晴らしいです！彼女の自己挿入小説を書こうと思います！ 

Romanization: "Watashi no tsuma wa totemo subarashīdesu! Kanojo no jiko nyūshō shōsetsu o kakou to omoimasu!"
What do you call a friend in Ukrainian?
In Ukrainian, a friend is called "друг" (druh).

Translate to English

"絵描きあるある"
"Common things among artists."

Translate to English

"もっと着ぐる絵頻繁に描こうと思っているんです。みんなは何を見るのが好きなんだろう？

ご希望が記載されていない場合は、リプでご指定ください。"

"I'm thinking of drawing more illustrations frequently, especially with winter clothing. I wonder what everyone likes to see?

If your request is not listed, please let me know in a reply."

Translate to English

"例、通常の着ぐるみ"
"For example, a regular costume."

Translate to English

"着ぐるみ衣裳は
着ぐるみマスクに合わせたものをオーダーしたいですね！！

ちなみに他社製、初代みらいちゃんです。"

"I would like to order a costume that matches the costume mask!!

By the way, this is a first-generation Mirai-chan from another company."

Translate to Japanese

"Mirai-chan costume"

"Mirai-chanのコスチューム"

Romanization: "Mirai-chan no kosuchūmu"

Translate to Japanese

“Mirai-chan kigurumi”

"未来ちゃんの着ぐるみ"

Romanization: "Mirai-chan no kigurumi"

Translate to English

"石川の皆さんに愛された「みらいちゃん」が、今日をもってMROを卒業します。
私が入社したときからずっと見守ってくれた、みらいちゃんでした。長い間、お疲れさまでした！！"

"Mirai-chan, who was loved by everyone in Ishikawa, graduated from MRO today.
She watched over me from the time I joined the company. Thank you for your long-standing service!!"

Translate to Japanese (with Romanization in parentheses)

"How I sleep knowing..."

"How I sleep knowing..."

Romanization: "Watashi ga shiru toki ni mata nemuru no wa..." (私が知るときにまた眠るのは...)
